mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO changes fail to associate with positive 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises discount rates and borrowing costs sufficiently to depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated inflation and restrictive short-term interest rates create a stagflationary discount where rising CPIAUCSL compresses real margins while elevated FEDFUNDS raises the real discount rate, jointly producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: high inflation with concurrent monetary tightening
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a negative signed Rank IC exceeding 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal does not underperform the equal-weighted single-signal baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of occurrences during late-cycle periods, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is not statistically negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential investment that spills over into construction employment and durable goods consumption, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1400, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When nominal retail sales (RSAFS) fail to keep pace with headline inflation (CPIAUCSL), the implied collapse in real consumer spending reduces corporate top-line growth, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction term fails to achieve a Rank IC magnitude above 0.03 with negative sign over any 3-year rolling window, or real consumption growth proxied by the spread does not negatively predict returns.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects aggregate labor income expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS changes remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll changes do not associate with positive 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms an economy-wide demand shortfall that reduces sales growth and operating leverage, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following quarters where GDPC1 growth is negative, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
